title: Tweezer-Array Wiring: Phase-Mask Hologram for a Qubit Register
strategies: plain superposition of gratings: place an in-phase unit point at every trap site, inverse-FFT, keep the phase (reproduces the evaluator's weak analytic baseline; coherent spot interference wrecks efficiency and uniformity)

random-phase superposition: give each trap an independent seeded random phase before the inverse transform, de-correlating the spots so far more of the beam lands in the traps, though depths stay uneven

weighted Gerchberg-Saxton: iterate propagate -> reweight dim/bright traps -> reinsert weighted achieved phase -> inverse-FFT, driving traps to equal depth while concentrating energy (high efficiency + near-perfect uniformity, still below the phase-only ideal)
seeded direct search / simulated annealing on the phase pixels, or adaptive-additive / mixed-region-amplitude-freedom variants that trade a controlled amount of uniformity for extra efficiency
